title: Tariri pentandra
taxonomy: Tariri pentandra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Diapensiaceae, a small family of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. It belongs to the genus Tariri, which is a genus of flowering plants. Tariri pentandra is also known as a type of diapensia. The classification of this plant is primarily based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features. Further research is needed to confirm its exact classification within the plant kingdom.
uses: Tariri pentandra, also known as the Canarium or Canarium tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The resin and leaves of the Tariri pentandra have been utilized to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In addition, the oil extracted from the tree has been used to manage skin conditions and wounds. The medicinal properties of Tariri pentandra may also help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Furthermore, some studies suggest that the tree's extracts have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
